persona_summary: Older, loyal customers with high promotion usage and visible technology and snack signals.
main_evidence: Mean age 60.4; mean spend 10,335; loyalty-card share 67.7%; children at home 1.6; groceries 43.8%, electronics 13.1%, vegetables 5.2%, meat 9.5%, basket coverage 83.9%.
recommended_promotion: Offer a tech-accessory bundle paired with energy drinks or snack bars, with a loyalty-card discount.
products_to_promote: airpods, bluetooth headphones, energy drink, energy bar, protein bar
supporting_products: airpods, laptop, bluetooth headphones, energy drink, energy bar
supporting_association_rules: energy drink -> bluetooth headphones (lift 2.57); bluetooth headphones -> energy drink (lift 2.57)
risk_or_caveat: Spend is lower than other mature segments, so keep discounts controlled.

persona_summary: The broad main segment, dominated by grocery spend and family-oriented fresh-food baskets.
main_evidence: Mean age 56.4; mean spend 25,883; loyalty-card share 61.1%; children at home 1.6; groceries 75.9%, electronics 6.7%, vegetables 2.4%, meat 3.3%, basket coverage 84.4%.
recommended_promotion: Use simple grocery and fresh-produce multi-buy offers for repeat basket items.
products_to_promote: asparagus, tomatoes, spinach, carrots, avocado, salad
risk_or_caveat: This is the largest and broadest cluster, so promotions should stay general.

persona_summary: Family households with the strongest vegetable share and very low meat and fish shares.
main_evidence: Mean age 60.6; mean spend 15,564; loyalty-card share 57.8%; children at home 1.9; groceries 57.4%, electronics 9.9%, vegetables 12.7%, meat 0.8%, basket coverage 89.1%.
recommended_promotion: Create a healthy family basket around vegetables, cooking basics, baby items, and pet essentials.
products_to_promote: asparagus, cooking oil, cereals, milk, babies food, dog food, cat food
supporting_products: dog food, babies food, napkins, cooking oil, cat food
supporting_association_rules: cooking oil -> napkins (lift 2.35); napkins -> cooking oil (lift 2.35)
risk_or_caveat: This is vegetarian-leaning, not strictly vegetarian, because some meat products still appear.

persona_summary: Lower-loyalty customers with the highest complaints, strong personal-care baskets, and the highest electronics share.
main_evidence: Mean age 59.5; mean spend 22,720; loyalty-card share 49.2%; children at home 0.7; groceries 36.4%, electronics 37.5%, vegetables 1.4%, meat 4.1%, basket coverage 84.0%.
recommended_promotion: Use a retention-focused personal-care replenishment bundle with optional tech-accessory cross-sell.
products_to_promote: toothpaste, deodorant, shampoo, shower gel, tooth brush, airpods
supporting_products: toothpaste, deodorant, shampoo, shower gel, tooth brush
supporting_association_rules: airpods -> bluetooth headphones (lift 3.83); bluetooth headphones -> airpods (lift 3.83)
risk_or_caveat: Use a professional label in the report; the informal Karen-like idea is only an interpretation of complaints and product behaviour.

persona_summary: The highest-spending and most tenured family segment, with very large households and breakfast/grocery baskets.
main_evidence: Mean age 60.4; mean spend 37,486; loyalty-card share 67.4%; children at home 6.4; groceries 65.2%, electronics 13.9%, vegetables 2.3%, meat 4.2%, basket coverage 84.8%.
recommended_promotion: Offer premium loyalty rewards and family breakfast bundles rather than deep discounts.
products_to_promote: cereals, fresh bread, butter, eggs, milk, honey, tea
supporting_products: cereals, fresh bread, butter, eggs, bacon
supporting_association_rules: mashed potato -> honey (lift 1.39); mushroom cream sauce -> bacon (lift 1.37)
risk_or_caveat: High-value customers may not need heavy discounts; focus on retention and value.
